NSHD Library File Topic Subtopic 1 Subtopic 2 Subtopic 3 Subtopic 4 NSHD Variable Name Showcase Field ID UKLLC Dataset Name(s) Variable Label Value labels Units Form Question Number Year of collection Is variable derived? Is variable sensitive? Reason variable is sensitive Notes
Myft_Soci Sub-Studies Myofit Physical capability aft_myft 70106 Is there anyone living with you who is sick, frail, or has a disability whom you look after or give special help to (for example, a sick, disabled, or older relative/husband/wife/friend, etc.)? - Myofit sub-study {-7.0} Not part of MyoFit46 {-8.0} Not applicable {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Myofit sub-study Not applicable 2020-2025 No Yes Low cell counts Full variable label: Is there anyone living with you who is sick, frail, or has a disability whom you look after or give special help to (for example, a sick, disabled, or older relative/husband/wife/friend, etc.)? - Myofit sub-study. Care giving/receiving. These variables need to be merged with those who have answered the questions in the 2023 Home visit (aft23x).
Myft_Soci Sub-Studies Myofit Physical capability aft1_myft 70107 Who do you look after or help that lives with you - Myofit sub-study {-7.0} Not part of MyoFit46 {-8.0} Not applicable {-9.0} Unknown {1.0} Wife/Husband/Partner {2.0} Mother/Mother-in-law {3.0} Father/Father-in-law {4.0} Son/Daughter {5.0} Grandchild/ren {6.0} Friend {7.0} Other Not applicable Myofit sub-study Not applicable 2020-2025 No Yes Low cell counts Care giving/receiving. These variables need to be merged with those who have answered the questions in the 2023 Home visit (aft123x_1, …, aft123x_7).

These variables need to be merged with those who have answered the questions in the 2023 Home visit (afth23x).

Myft_ACE Sub-Studies Myofit Cognitive function ACE-III (cognitive state) ace_date_myft 70003 Date of ACE-III assessment - Myofit sub-study. {-9.0} Unknown date ACEIII questionnaire Not applicable 2020-2025 Yes No For those participants who were unable to do the ACE test during the 2023 Home visit, they were asked to do the test during the Myofit clinic visit. These need to be merged with the 2023 HV and NOT the 2015 HV.

Myft_Soci Sub-Studies Myofit Sociodemographics Economic circumstances incass_myft 70105 If you sold all the assets you own in your household, e.g your house, car, jewellery, cashed in your savings and investments, and paid off any debts you have, how much money do you think you would have - Myofit sub-study {-7.0} Not part of MyoFit46 {-8.0} Not applicable {-9.0} Unknown {1.0} Less than ?12,500 {10.0} ?700,000 - ?799,999 {11.0} ?800,000 - ?899,999 {12.0} ?900,000 - ?999,999 {13.0} ?1,000,000 - ?1,999,999 {14.0} 2,000,000 or more {2.0} ?12,500 - ?39,999 {3.0} ?40,000 - ?99,999 {4.0} ?100,000 - ?199,999 {5.0} ?200,000 - ?299,999 {6.0} ?300,000 - ?399,999 {7.0} ?400,000 - ?499,999 {8.0} ?500,000 - ?599,999 {9.0} ?600,000 - ?699,999 Not applicable Myofit sub-study Not applicable 2020-2025 No Yes Low cell counts Full variable label: If you sold all the assets you own in your household, e.g your house, car, jewellery, cashed in your savings and investments, and paid off any debts you have, how much money do you think you would have - Myofit sub-study. Socioeconomic position. These variables need to be merged with those who have answered the questions in the 2023 Home visit.

I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Population characteristics Type of visit remote_type_i46p2 61652 Type of remote assessment (telephone or video) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} Telephone {1.0} Video Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 type of assessment: the study protocol was adapted during the coronavirus pandemic when it was not possible to see participants in-person to allow completion of some tests by remote assessment, either by video or telephone.

I46_Cortical_thickness Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Cortical thickness lh_adh_corthck_i46p1 60226 Left hemisphere ADsig Harvard cortical thickness (mm) - Longitudinal - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown mm Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 cortical thickness assessed using Freesurfer's longitudinal stream in two composite regions of interest: one comprised of middle temporal, inferior temporal, entorhinal and fusiform areas (ADsig Mayo); and one consisting of parahippocampal, inferior temporal, entorhinal, temporal pole, precuneus, supramarginal, superior and inferior parietal, superior frontal, parsopercularis, parstriangularis and parsorbitalis areas (ADsig Harvard). Also measured in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_Cortical_thickness.
I46P2_Cortical_thickness Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Cortical thickness lh_adh_corthck_i46p2 60226 Left hemisphere ADsig Harvard cortical thickness (mm) - Longitudinal - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown mm Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 cortical thickness assessed using Freesurfer's longitudinal stream in two composite regions of interest: one comprised of middle temporal, inferior temporal, entorhinal and fusiform areas (ADsig Mayo); and one consisting of parahippocampal, inferior temporal, entorhinal, temporal pole, precuneus, supramarginal, superior and inferior parietal, superior frontal, parsopercularis, parstriangularis and parsorbitalis areas (ADsig Harvard). Also measured in phase 1 - see library file I46_Cortical_thickness.

I46_Cortical_thickness Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Cortical thickness mean_adm_corthck_i46p1 60236 Surface area weighted average of left and right hemisphere ADsig Mayo cortical thickness (mm) - Longitudinal - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown mm Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 cortical thickness assessed using Freesurfer's longitudinal stream in two composite regions of interest: one comprised of middle temporal, inferior temporal, entorhinal and fusiform areas (ADsig Mayo); and one consisting of parahippocampal, inferior temporal, entorhinal, temporal pole, precuneus, supramarginal, superior and inferior parietal, superior frontal, parsopercularis, parstriangularis and parsorbitalis areas (ADsig Harvard). Also measured in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_Cortical_thickness.
I46P2_Cortical_thickness Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Cortical thickness mean_adm_corthck_i46p2 60236 Surface area weighted average of left and right hemisphere ADsig Mayo cortical thickness (mm) - Longitudinal - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown mm Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 cortical thickness assessed using Freesurfer's longitudinal stream in two composite regions of interest: one comprised of middle temporal, inferior temporal, entorhinal and fusiform areas (ADsig Mayo); and one consisting of parahippocampal, inferior temporal, entorhinal, temporal pole, precuneus, supramarginal, superior and inferior parietal, superior frontal, parsopercularis, parstriangularis and parsorbitalis areas (ADsig Harvard). Also measured in phase 1 - see library file I46_Cortical_thickness.
I46_Brainage Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain age brn_age_i46p1 60238 Age at brain scan (years) - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} No brain scan years Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No Yes Contains age information. Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control.
I46_Brainage Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain age brn_bpa_i46p1 60239 Adjusted baseline brain-predicted age - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} No brain scan years Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes Yes Contains age information. Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control. The bl_bpa_adjusted variable is the brain-predicted age after adjustment for age bias. The age bias adjustment was performed by subtracting 3.33 from the unadjusted brain age and dividing by 0.91. These values were the intercept and slope of the model in the training dataset of brain-predicted age regressed on chronological age.
I46_Brainage Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain age brn_bpaun_i46p1 60240 Unadjusted baseline brain-predicted age - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} No brain scan years Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes Yes Contains age information. Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control.
I46_Brainage Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain age brn_mri_i46p1 60241 Did the participant have a brain scan - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control.
I46_Brainage Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain age brn_pad_i46p1 60242 Adjusted baseline brain predicted age differeince (Brain-PAD) - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} No brain scan years Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes Yes Contains age information. Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control. Brain-predicted age difference (Brain-PAD) is provided for the adjusted brain-predicted age estimates only. This was calculated by subtracting chronological age from adjusted brain-predicted age.
I46P2_BP Sub-Studies Insight46 Physical measures Blood pressure sit_syst1_i46p2 61429 First sitting systolic blood pressure after 3 minutes of rest (mmHg) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown mmHg Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 blood pressure and pulse rate measures. Measurements were performed twice (one minute apart) in the left arm after 3 minutes of sitting down, and then twice (one minute apart) following 3 minutes in the recumbent position, and then twice (one minute apart) following 3 minutes of standing quietly. Lying and standing measures were also taken in phase 1 - see library file I46_bloodPressure.
I46P2_BP Sub-Studies Insight46 Physical measures Blood pressure sit_syst2_i46p2 61430 Second sitting systolic blood pressure (mmHg) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown mmHg Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 blood pressure and pulse rate measures. Measurements were performed twice (one minute apart) in the left arm after 3 minutes of sitting down, and then twice (one minute apart) following 3 minutes in the recumbent position, and then twice (one minute apart) following 3 minutes of standing quietly. Lying and standing measures were also taken in phase 1 - see library file I46_bloodPressure.

I46_WordInNoise Sub-Studies Insight46 Physical measures Hearing test nal_complete_i46p1 61020 Whether National Acoustics Laboratories-Revised (NAL-R) linear prescriptive formula (NAL) was completed or not - word in noise - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 word in noise. High-frequency monosyllabic words are presented against background noise, and the sound level of each word is varied, to obtain a "speech reception threshold" which represents the ability of the participant to identify words in background noise . Not collected at phase 2.

High-frequency monosyllabic words are presented against background noise, and the sound level of each word is varied, to obtain a "speech reception threshold" which represents the ability of the participant to identify words in background noise . Not collected at phase 2.

I46_braintap Sub-Studies Insight46 Physical activity Bradykinesia Akinesia Incoordination (BRAIN) Tap Test bt_ds_lft15_i46p1 60647 Dysmetria score (DS) - Weighted index of number of incorrect key taps, scored in a target fashion, then divided by the total number of key taps - left hand- Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {999.0} Missing Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 BRAIN test: a validated test for detecting signs of Parkinson's disease and other neurological conditions. Using a Lenovo Thinkpad laptop, participants were instructed to tap as fast and accurately as possible, alternating between the s and ; keys for thirty seconds, with each hand separately. In several cases, the hand used for each test may have been recorded incorrectly (indicated by a discrepancy between the data recorded by staff and the website). This discrepancy has been coded with the variable “BT_rightleftuncertain_15“,the data is useable for analyses using a the mean of both hands, or “worst hand” analyses, but should be excluded if relationships with dominant or non-dominant hands are investigated. Also performed at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_braintap.
I46P2_braintap Sub-Studies Insight46 Physical activity Bradykinesia Akinesia Incoordination (BRAIN) Tap Test bt_ds_lft15_i46p2 60647 Dysmetria score (DS) - Weighted index of number of incorrect key taps, scored in a target fashion, then divided by the total number of key taps - left hand- Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {999.0} Missing Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 BRAIN test: a validated test for detecting signs of Parkinson's disease and other neurological conditions. In several cases, the hand used for each test may have been recorded incorrectly (indicated by a discrepancy between the data recorded by staff and the website). This discrepancy has been coded with the variable “BT_rightleftuncertain_15“,the data is useable for analyses using a the mean of both hands, or “worst hand” analyses, but should be excluded if relationships with dominant or non-dominant hands are investigated. Also performed at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_braintap.

I46P2_braintap Sub-Studies Insight46 Physical activity Bradykinesia Akinesia Incoordination (BRAIN) Tap Test bt_at_mean15_i46p2 60656 Akinesia time (AT) - Mean dwell time on each key (milliseconds) - mean of left and right hands - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {999.0} Missing milliseconds Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 BRAIN test: a validated test for detecting signs of Parkinson's disease and other neurological conditions. Using a Lenovo Thinkpad laptop, participants were instructed to tap as fast and accurately as possible, alternating between the s and ; keys for thirty seconds, with each hand separately. In several cases, the hand used for each test may have been recorded incorrectly (indicated by a discrepancy between the data recorded by staff and the website). This discrepancy has been coded with the variable “BT_rightleftuncertain_15“,the data is useable for analyses using a the mean of both hands, or “worst hand” analyses, but should be excluded if relationships with dominant or non-dominant hands are investigated. Also performed at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_braintap.

I46_Gait Sub-Studies Insight46 Physical activity Gait assessment w1t1_betadeg_i46p1 60672 Walk 1, Task 1 - Beta (deg) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown deg Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 instrumented 10 metre gait assessment. Repeated in Insight46 phase 2 - see library file I46P2_Gait.

I46P2_Lumipulse Sub-Studies Insight46 Biological samples Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers ab40_lumi_i46p2 61071 Ab40 biomarker measured in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) using Lumipulse assays - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Unknown pg/ml Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 CSF biomarker analysis - details of the commercially available Lumipulse AB40, AB42, p-tau181 and t-tau assays are given in the kit inserts and they were all used as per the manufacturer’s instructions. Measurements were undertaken in singlicate, using two reagent batches: the first 72 samples were assayed in 2019 for an interim analysis (1) and the remaining 62 samples were assayed in 2021. CSF was not collected at phase 1.
I46P2_Lumipulse Sub-Studies Insight46 Biological samples Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers ab42_lumi_i46p2 61072 Ab42 biomarker measured in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) using Lumipulse assays - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Unknown pg/ml Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 CSF biomarker analysis - details of the commercially available Lumipulse AB40, AB42, p-tau181 and t-tau assays are given in the kit inserts and they were all used as per the manufacturer’s instructions. Measurements were undertaken in singlicate, using two reagent batches: the first 72 samples were assayed in 2019 for an interim analysis (1) and the remaining 62 samples were assayed in 2021. CSF was not collected at phase 1.

I46_apoe Sub-Studies Insight46 Genomics Genotype ins_apoe_i46p1 61079 ApoE allele status from 60-64, or Insight 46 [69-71yrs] if not known at earlier - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} missing {1.0} e2/e2 {2.0} e2/e3 {3.0} e2/e4 {4.0} e3/e3 {5.0} e3/e4 {6.0} e4/e4 Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No APOE genotype was determined using the two SNPs, rs439358 and rs7412. Individuals were coded according to the presence of e2, e3 and e4 alleles.

I46_BRAINDISORDER Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder mbraindis_i46p1 61224 Classified as having a major brain disorder - Insight46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 major brain disorder were determined by a consensus panel including consultant neurologists and study clinicians who had reviewed the participants. Decisions were made based on self-reported diagnosis, radiological reads, and clinician opinion.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder mjrbraindis_i46p2 61224 Classified as having a major brain disorder - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 major brain disorder were determined by a consensus panel including consultant neurologists and study clinicians who had reviewed the participants. Decisions were made based on self-reported diagnosis, radiological reads, and clinician opinion. It includes participants defined as having a major brain disorder at phase 1 (see library file I46_BRAINDISORDER)
I46_BRAINDISORDER Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder diag1_brdis_i46p1 61225 Specifies diagnosis if yes to major brain disorder - Insight46 Phase 1 {-88.0} Not applicable {-9.0} Unknown {1.0} Radiological cortical stroke {10.0} Subdural haematomas requiring neurosurgery {11.0} Myotonic dystrophy {12.0} Parkinson's disease {13.0} Depression requiring electroconvulsive therapy {14.0} Traumatic brain injury {15.0} Hepatic encephalopathy {16.0} Radiological multiple sclerosis {2.0} Bipolar disorder requiring anti-psychotic medication {3.0} Clinical stroke {4.0} Dementia not otherwise specified {5.0} Epilepsy requiring active treatment {6.0} Clinical stroke and radiological cortical stroke {7.0} Brain metastasis {8.0} Alzheimer's Disease {9.0} Multiple Sclerosis Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No Yes Text variables, may contain sensitive information Insight46 phase 1 major brain disorder were determined by a consensus panel including consultant neurologists and study clinicians who had reviewed the participants. Decisions were made based on self-reported diagnosis, radiological reads, and clinician opinion.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder diag1_cog_i46p2 61225 Specifies diagnosis if yes to major brain disorder - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes Text variables, may contain sensitive information Insight46 phase 2 major brain disorder were determined by a consensus panel including consultant neurologists and study clinicians who had reviewed the participants. Decisions were made based on self-reported diagnosis, radiological reads, and clinician opinion. It includes participants defined as having a major brain disorder at phase 1 (see library file I46_BRAINDISORDER)
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder participant_concern_i46p1 61274 Does the participant have concerns regarding their cognition (from subjective cognitive decline questions)? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Participant concern regarding their cognition (memory or cognitive difficulties more than other people the same age, or if they felt they would seek medical attention regarding their difficulties).
I46_BRAINDISORDER Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder diag2_brdis_i46p1 61226 Specifies second diagnosis if more than one major brain disorder - Insight46 phase 1 {-88.0} Not applicable {-9.0} Unknown {1.0} Epilepsy requiring active treatment Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No Yes Text variables, may contain sensitive information Insight46 phase 1 major brain disorder were determined by a consensus panel including consultant neurologists and study clinicians who had reviewed the participants. Decisions were made based on self-reported diagnosis, radiological reads, and clinician opinion.
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder dementia_consensus_i46p1 61275 Does the participant have a consensus diagnosis of dementia? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment/dementia. Dementia was determined at an adjudication meeting involving study doctors, psychologists and other team members who assessed the participant. It was based on self-reported history by the participant, informant history, clinician assessment and performance on cognitive testing. Also assessed at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_Cognitive.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder dementia_i46p2 61648 Defined as having dementia - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 dementia status was determined based on self-reported history by the participant, informant history, clinician assessment and performance on cognitive testing, and included participants who were defined as having dementia in phase 1 - see library file I46_memory.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder diag2_cog_i46p2 61226 Specifies second diagnosis if more than one major brain disorder - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes Text variables, may contain sensitive information Insight46 phase 2 major brain disorder were determined by a consensus panel including consultant neurologists and study clinicians who had reviewed the participants. Decisions were made based on self-reported diagnosis, radiological reads, and clinician opinion. It includes participants defined as having a major brain disorder at phase 1 (see library file I46_BRAINDISORDER)
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder informant_concern_i46p1 61276 Does the informant have concerns regarding the participant's cognition (from AD8 questionnaire)? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Informant concern regarding the participant’s cognition (AD8 score ≥ 2).
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder low_digit_symbol_i46p1 61277 Did this participant have a low score (<= -1.5 z-score) on the Logical Memory Delayed recall test? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Objective evidence of an amnestic deficit (Logical Memory delayed recall ≥ 1.5 SD below the mean). This cognitive test was chosen for defining a cognitive deficit on the basis of its normal distribution across the entire cohort. Also collected during phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_MMSE.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder diag3_cog_i46p2 61647 Specifies third diagnosis if more than two major brain disorders - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes Text variables, may contain sensitive information Insight46 phase 2 major brain disorder were determined by a consensus panel including consultant neurologists and study clinicians who had reviewed the participants. Decisions were made based on self-reported diagnosis, radiological reads, and clinician opinion. It includes participants defined as having a major brain disorder at phase 1 (see library file I46_BRAINDISORDER)
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder low_logical_memory_i46p1 61278 Did this participant have a low score (<= -1.5 z-score) on the Digit-symbol substitution test? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Objective evidence of a non-amnestic deficit (Digit-Symbol Substitution score ≥ 1.5 SD below the mean). This cognitive test was chosen for defining a cognitive deficit on the basis of its normal distribution across the entire cohort. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_MMSE.
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder mci_i46p1 61279 Participant meets criteria for Mild Cognitive Impairment - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Criteria for Mild cognitive impairment (MCI) were agreed based on published criteria. The principle of these criteria is that they require an absence of dementia AND evidence of objective cognitive impairment on one or more neuropsychological tests AND subjective cognitive concerns reported by the participant or an informant. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_Cognitive.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder mci_i46p2 61279 Participant meets criteria for Mild Cognitive Impairment - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 mild cognitive impairment. Criteria for mild cognitive impairment (MCI) were agreed based on published criteria. The principle of these criteria is that they require an absence of dementia AND evidence of objective cognitive impairment on one or more neuropsychological tests AND subjective cognitive concerns reported by the participant or an informant. It was determined using phase 2 data only. Also derived at phase 1 - see library file I46_memory.
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder mycog_phone_i46p1 61239 I find it harder to learn new telephone numbers - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 subjective cognitive decline, measured by the SCD-Q part 1 (MyCog Questionnaire). Participants are instructed to answer YES if they believe they perform these activities WORSE than roughly two years ago. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_memory.
I46P2_Memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder phone_i46p2 61239 I find it harder to learn new telephone numbers - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 subjective cognitive decline, measured by the SCD-Q part 1 (MyCog Questionnaire). Participants are instructed to answer YES if they believe they perform these activities WORSE than roughly two years ago. Also collected in phase 1 - see library file I46_memory.

Participants are instructed to answer YES if they believe they perform these activities WORSE than roughly two years ago. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_memory.

I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder scd_difficulties_i46p1 61264 Do you perceive memory or cognitive difficulties? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 subjective cognitive decline questions, designsed to asssess for SCD-plus features which increase the likelihood of preclinical Alzheimer's disease (see Table 3 in Rami et al 2014 doi: 10.1016/j.jalz.2014.01.001) . Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_Memory.

I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Personal history of neurological disorder scd_doctor_i46p1 61270 If yes to either scd_difficulties_i46p1 or scd_twoyears_i46p1 , would you ask a doctor about these difficulties? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 subjective cognitive decline questions, designsed to asssess for SCD-plus features which increase the likelihood of preclinical Alzheimer's disease (see Table 3 in Rami et al 2014 doi: 10.1016/j.jalz.2014.01.001) . Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_Memory.

I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_medusge_i46p1 61213 Whether any medication used (not limited to vascular medication) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.
I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_medhtn_i46p1 61214 Presence of Antihypertensive medication. These drugs are prescribed for high blood pressure or heart failure and includes angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors (BNF255). Includes BNF groups 2.5.1, 2.5.2, 2.5.3, 2.5.4, 2.5.5, 2.5.5.1, 2.5.5.2 & 2.5.5.3. - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No medication {1.0} Presence of medication Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.
I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_meddiuretic_i46p1 61215 Presence of Diuretics. These drugs may be prescribed to promote renal function and offload fluid (for example in heart failure) or to lower blood pressure. The group includes thiazide like diuretics (BNF221), loop diuretics (BNF222), potassium sparing diuretics (BNF223, BNF224) and other diuretics. Includes BNF groups 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4, 2.2.5, 2.2.6, 2.2.7 & 2.2.8. - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No medication {1.0} Presence of medication Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.
I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_medbeta_i46p1 61216 Presence of Beta Blockers. These drugs may be prescribed for heart failure, tachycardia, anxiety, hypertension or ischaemic heart disease. Includes BNF group 2.4. - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No medication {1.0} Presence of medication Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.
I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_mednitrateccb_i46p1 61217 Presence of Nitrates. These drugs may be prescribed to lower blood pressure, or in heart failure or ischaemic heart disease. It includes calcium channel blockers (BNF262). Includes BNF groups 2.6.1, 2.6.2, 2.6.3 & 2.6.4. - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No medication {1.0} Presence of medication Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.
I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_bplwrmed_total_i46p1 61218 Total number of potential antihypertensive medications being taken. Derived from original data. - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} Zero {1.0} One {2.0} Two {3.0} Three {4.0} Four {5.0} Five Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes Yes Possible low cell counts issue Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.
I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_cholmed_i46p1 61219 Total number of lipid-regulating medications being taken. Based on BNF group 2.12. - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} Zero {1.0} One {2.0} Two Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes Yes Possible low cell counts issue Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.
I46_Vascular_medication Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Medications vasc_dmmed_i46p1 61220 Total number of diabetic medications being taken. Based on BNF groups 6.1.1 (insulins) and 6.1.2 (oral antidiabetic drugs). - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} Zero {1.0} One {2.0} Two {3.0} Three Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes Yes Possible low cell counts issue Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.

Presence of any potential blood pressure lowering medication. Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.

Vascular medications (antihypertensive medication, diabetic and lipid-regulating medication usage) were then coded in greater detail based on BNF groups.

I46P2_Head_injury Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Head injury headinj_age_i46p2 61280 Age of head injury questionnaire - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown years Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes Contains age information. This longer head injury self-complete questionnaire was introduced to Insight 46 during phase 2. Limited head injury questions were asked during the phase 1 clinical interview (see file handle I46_familyHistory), and were repeated during the phase 2 clinical interview (see file handle I462_history).
I46P2_Head_injury Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Head injury headinj_comp_i46p2 61281 Completed head injury questionnaire - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No This longer head injury self-complete questionnaire was introduced to Insight 46 during phase 2. Limited head injury questions were asked during the phase 1 clinical interview (see file handle I46_familyHistory), and were repeated during the phase 2 clinical interview (see file handle I462_history).

I46_selfCompletion Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Dental health rinsing_i46p1 61331 Apart from brushing, how many times in the past 7 days did you use mouthwash or other dental rinse product that you use to treat dental disease or dental problems? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} Zero {1.0} One {2.0} Two {3.0} Three {4.0} Four {5.0} Five {6.0} Six {7.0} Seven days Insight46 - Phase 1 48 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 self completion questionnaire - sleep and dental questions; not repeated at phase 2.
I46_selfCompletion Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep actoutdreams_i46p1 61332 Have you ever been told, or suspected, that you act out your dreams while asleep (for example, punching, flailing your arms in the air, making running movements, etc)? - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {9.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 49 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 self completion questionnaire - sleep and dental questions; not repeated at phase 2.
I46_selfCompletion Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep totalhandedness_i46p1 61334 Total-handedness summary score (higher score=more right-handed) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 self completion questionnaire - sleep and dental questions; not repeated at phase 2.
I46P2_epworth Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep age_epi46p2 61336 Age - Epsworth Sleepiness Scale - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Years Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes Contains age information. Insight46 phase 1 self completion questionnaire - The State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI). Maximum score is 80. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_StatAnx.
I46P2_epworth Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep totalscore_epi46p2 61337 Summed score - total score out of 24 - Epsworth Sleepiness Scale - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Epworth Sleepiness Scale - participants were asked to rate how likely they are to fall asleep in eight different situations, and the score from each situation was summed to give a total score out of 24. A total score of 16 or more indicates a high level of daytime sleepiness. Not administered at phase 1.
I46P2_epworth Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep completed_epi46p2 61338 Completed - Epsworth Sleepiness Scale - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Epworth Sleepiness Scale - participants were asked to rate how likely they are to fall asleep in eight different situations, and the score from each situation was summed to give a total score out of 24. A total score of 16 or more indicates a high level of daytime sleepiness. Not administered at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep bedtime_i46p2 61340 PSQI Q1: During past month, what time have you usually gone to bed at night? - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes May contain sensitive information Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep timetoslp_i46p2 61341 PSQI Q2: During past month, how long (in minutes) has it usually taken you to fall asleep - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing minutes Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.

A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.

I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep enthusiasm_i46p2 61359 PSQI Q9: During past month, how difficult to keep up enough enthusiasm - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} Not during the past month {1.0} less than once a week {2.0} 1-2 per week {3.0} >2 per week Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep bedpartner_i46p2 61360 PSQI Q10: Do you have a bed partner or housemate? - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} No bed partner or room mate {1.0} Partner/housemate in other room {2.0} Partner in same room but diff bed {3.0} Partner in same bed Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes May contain sensitive information Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep psqi_i46p2 61361 PSQI global score (psqidurat_i46p2 + psqidistb_i46p2 + psqilaten_i46p2 + psqidaysdys_i46p2 + psqihse_i46p2 + sleepqltyrtng_i46p2 + medsleep_i46p2). Min score = 0 (better), max = 21 (worse). Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep psqidaysdys_i46p2 61362 Days of dysfunction due to sleepiness. Categorized such that if totalscore_i46p2 is = 0 then psqidaysdys_i46p2 = 0, ≥ 1 and ≤ 2 = 1, ≥ 3 and ≤ 4 = 2, and ≥ 5 and ≤ 6 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqidaysdys_i46p2 variable is known as PSQIDAYDYS. - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} Better {1.0} One {2.0} Two {3.0} Worse Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep psqidistb_i46p2 61363 Sleep disturbance. Categorized such that if sumscore_i46p2 = 0 then psqidistb_i46p2 = 0, ≥ 1 and ≤ 9 = 1, > 9 and ≤ 18 = 2, and > 18 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqidistb_i46p2 variable is known as PSQIDISTB. - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} Better {1.0} One {2.0} Two {3.0} Worse Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep psqidurat_i46p2 61364 Sleep duration. Categorized such that if actslpdur_i46p2 ≥ 7 then psqidurat_i46p2 = 0, < 7 and ≥ 6 = 1, <6 and ≥ 5 = 2, and < 5 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqidurat_i46p2 variable is known as PSQUDURAT. - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} >=7 {1.0} >=6 and <7 {2.0} >=5 and <6 {3.0} <5 hours Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep psqihse_i46p2 61365 Sleep efficiency. Categorized such that if tmphse_i46p2 is ≥ 85 then psqihse_i46p2 = 0, < 85 and ≥ 75 = 1, <75 and ≥ 65 = 2, and < 65 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqihse_i46p2 variable is known as PSQIHSE. - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} >85 {1.0} >=75 and <85 {2.0} >=65 and <75 {3.0} <65 Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep psqilaten_i46p2 61366 Sleep latency. Categorized such that if timetoslpcat_i46p2 is = 0 then psqilaten_i46p2 = 0, ≥ 1 and ≤ 2 = 1, ≥ 3 and ≤ 4 = 2, and ≥ 5 and ≤ 6 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqilaten_i46p2 variable is known as PSQILATEN . - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} Better {1.0} One {2.0} Two {3.0} Worse Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep slpdur_i46p2 61367 Derived by finding difference in seconds between bedtime_i46p2 and day wakeuptime_i46p2 and dividing by 3600 to get difference in hours. If >24, then slpdur_i46p2 = difference in hours minus 24, if ≤24, then slpdur_i46p2 = difference in hours. In PSQI scoring tool, slpdur_i46p2 variable is nown as newtib. - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing hours Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep sumscore_i46p2 61368 Total sleep disturbance score derived by summation of Q5(b) to Q5(j) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep timetoslpcat_i46p2 61369 Categorized such that if timetoslp_i46p2 ≥ 0 and ≤15 then timetoslpcat_i46p2 = 0, >15 and ≤ 30 = 1, >30 and ≤ 60 = 2, and >60 = 3. In PSQI scoring tool, this variable is known as Q2new - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} <=15 {1.0} 16-30 {2.0} 31-60 {3.0} >60 minutes Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep tmphse_i46p2 61370 Percentage of total time spent sleeping in bed. Derived by (actslpdur_i46p2 / slpdur_i46p2) *100. In PSQI scoring tool, tmphse_i46p2 variable is known as tmphse. - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing % Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep totalscore_i46p2 61371 Summation of Q8 and Q9 scores - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} Zero {1.0} One {2.0} Two {3.0} Three Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep age_psqi_i46p2 61372 Age at visit - Pittsburgh sleep quality index (PSQI) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown years Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes Contains age information. Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. I46P2_PSQI Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep comp_psqi_i46p2 61373 Whether completed Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index questions - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_rem Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep disease_remi46p2 61375 First condition of the nervous system - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 REM sleep behaviour disorder questionnaire. A yes-no questionnaire, with each "yes" answer worth one point, resulting in a total score beween 0 and 13. A score of 5 or greater is indicative of REM sleep behaviour disorder. Not performed at phase 1.
I46P2_rem Sub-Studies Insight46 Health and medical history Sleep disease2_remi46p2 61376 Second condition of the nervous system - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 REM sleep behaviour disorder questionnaire. A yes-no questionnaire, with each "yes" answer worth one point, resulting in a total score beween 0 and 13. A score of 5 or greater is indicative of REM sleep behaviour disorder. Not performed at phase 1.

I46_selfCompletion Sub-Studies Insight46 Mental health Anxiety stateanxietyrec_i46p1 61383 Current state of anxiety (higher score=more anxious) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {45.0} 45 or over Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 self completion questionnaire - The State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI). Maximum score is 80. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_StatAnx.
I46_selfCompletion Sub-Studies Insight46 Mental health Anxiety traitanxietyrec_i46p1 61384 Anxiety as a personality trait (higher score=more anxious) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {50.0} 50 or over Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 self completion questionnaire - The State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI). Insight46 phase 2 self completion questionnaire - The State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI). Maximum score is 80. Also collected in phase 1 - see library file I46_selfCompletion

I46PTQ Sub-Studies Insight46 Mental health Negative thinking summedscore_ptqi46p2 61391 SummedScore (total score out of 60) - Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ): assesses repetitive negative thinking; consists of 15 statements, and participants are asked to rate the extent to which each statement applies to them on a scale from 0 (never) to 4 (almost always). Scores are summed to give a total score which ranges from 0 to 60. The PTQ was introduced part-way through data collection for phase 2. It was not performed in phase 1.
I46PTQ Sub-Studies Insight46 Mental health Negative thinking age_ptqi46p2 61392 Age at study visit - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown years Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No Yes Contains age information. Insight46 phase 2 Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ): assesses repetitive negative thinking; consists of 15 statements, and participants are asked to rate the extent to which each statement applies to them on a scale from 0 (never) to 4 (almost always). Scores are summed to give a total score which ranges from 0 to 60. The PTQ was introduced part-way through data collection for phase 2. It was not performed in phase 1.
I46PTQ Sub-Studies Insight46 Mental health Negative thinking completed_ptqi46p2 61393 Whether completed Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ) measure - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ): assesses repetitive negative thinking; consists of 15 statements, and participants are asked to rate the extent to which each statement applies to them on a scale from 0 (never) to 4 (almost always). Scores are summed to give a total score which ranges from 0 to 60. The PTQ was introduced part-way through data collection for phase 2. It was not performed in phase 1.
I46_memory Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Informant interviews ad8_appointments_i46p1 61395 AD8 informant questionnaire: Trouble remembering appointments - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Missing value {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Not known Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 AD8 - a short interview designed to be administered to a participant's informant to distinguish those with very mild dementia from those without dementia. A response of “Yes, a change” indicates that there has been a change in the last several years caused by cognitive (thinking and memory) problems. An AD8 total score (ad8_totrec_i46p1) greater than or equal to 2 indicates that cognitive impairment is likely to be present. Also collected at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_AD8.

I46P2_CDR Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Informant interviews cdr_score_i46p2 61419 Overall Clinical dementia rating (CDR) score - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 clinical dementia rating (CDR) - a global scale which assesses dementia severity via an interview with participant and informant. Performance is rated in six domains (memory, orientation, judgement and problem solving, community activities, home and hobbies, and personal care) and then a global CDR score is calculated by combining the scores from each domain using an establish scoring algorithm. Not measured at phase 1.
I46P2_CDR Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Informant interviews community_affairs_i46p2 61420 Community affairs - Clinical dementia rating (CDR) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 clinical dementia rating (CDR) - a global scale which assesses dementia severity via an interview with participant and informant. I46_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Mini-mental state examination mmse_year_i46p1 61600 MMSE: orientation - what is the year - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} Incorrect answer/response {1.0} Correct answer/response Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 Mini Mental State Examination (MMSE): a 30-point composite screening tool for cognitive impairment which is widely used within clinical practice. I46_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Reaction time reaction_mrt_i46p1 61462 Choice reaction time (RT): Mean RT for correct trials - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Invalid reaction time data milliseconds Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 Two related tasks – Choice Reaction Time (RT) and Response Inhibition – were combined into one experiment. The Choice RT task had 2 blocks of 12 trials each. The Response Inhibition task were 2 blocks of 48 trials each. Also collected at phase 2 - see I46P2_NeuroPsyc_ChoiceRT and I46P2_NeuroPsyc_RespInhib.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Reaction time rmrt_i46p2 61462 Choice reaction time (RT): Mean RT for correct trials - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown milliseconds Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Two related tasks – Choice Reaction Time (RT) and Response Inhibition – were combined into one experiment. The Choice RT task had 2 blocks of 12 trials each. The Response Inhibition task were 2 blocks of 48 trials each. For both tasks, participants were instructed to press the correct button on the response box (left or right) as quickly as possible, according to the stimulus on the screen. Also collected at phase 1 - see I46_neuropsychology.

The Choice RT task had 2 blocks of 12 trials each. The Response Inhibition task was 2 blocks of 48 trials each. Also collected at phase 1 - see I46_neuropsychology.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Reaction time scomp_respi46p2 61477 Switching: completed? - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Unable for health reasons {3.0} Unable, other Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Two related tasks – Choice Reaction Time (RT) and Response Inhibition – were combined into one experiment. I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Reaction time scomp_i46p2 61646 Switching: completed? - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Unable for health reasons {3.0} Unable, other Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Two related tasks – Choice Reaction Time (RT) and Response Inhibition – were combined into one experiment. The Choice RT task had 2 blocks of 12 trials each. The Response Inhibition task were 2 blocks of 48 trials each. For both tasks, participants were instructed to press the correct button on the response box (left or right) as quickly as possible, according to the stimulus on the screen. Also collected at phase 1 - see I46_neuropsychology.

I46_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Visuomotor performance circle_rateo_i46p1 61481 Circle tracing: mean subtraction rate (responses per second) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown response/sec Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 circle-tracing and serial subtraction task. Also collected at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_CircleTracing.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Visuomotor performance crateo_i46p2 61481 Circle tracing: mean subtraction rate (responses per second) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown response/sec Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 circle-tracing and serial subtraction task. Participants were instructed to trace clockwise round the circle using a stylus as quickly and accurately as possible. In the direct condition, participants could see their hand and the path they were tracing on the tablet screen. In the indirect condition, the laptop was covered by an upturned box with the front open to allow the participant to put their hand inside.The task was administered in a single-task format (no subtraction) and a dual-task format with a concurrent task of serial subtraction. Also collected at phase 1 - see library file I46_neuropsychology
I46_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Visuomotor performance circle_errn_i46p1 61482 Circle tracing: total number of incorrect subtraction responses summed across all 6 trials - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 circle-tracing and serial subtraction task. I46_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Associative episodic memory fname_crn_i46p1 61506 Face-Name test (FNAME): CRN Cued Recall Names short delay - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 Face-Name test (FNAME-12) assesses associative memory for face-name and face-occupation pairs. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_MMSE. Two versions exist: FNAME-12A and FNAME12-B. Phase 1 and 2 both used FNAME-12A.

I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Visuo-constructional ability and visual memory acomp_i46p2 61571 Adult Memory and Information Processing Battery (AMIPB):was the participant willing to complete the AMIPB figure? - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes {2.0} Unable for health reasons {3.0} Unable, other Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 AMIPB complex figure drawing test of visual memory recall. Participants are asked to copy a complex figure as accurately as possible, and then to draw it from memory immediately afterwards, at 30 minutes, and at 7 days. Figure version 1 was used in phase 2. Not performed in phase 1.

I46_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) pacc_i46p1 61649 Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) - The four components of our version of the PACC were: MMSE total score, Logical Memory delayed recall score, Digit-Symbol Substitution score and FNAME-total. The four components were converted into z-scores based on the full Insight 46 sample and the four z-scores were then averaged. This has been recalculated at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_MMSE.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) pacc_i46p2 61650 Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (based on distributions from Phase 1) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) - The four components of our version of the PACC were: MMSE total score, Logical Memory delayed recall score, Digit-Symbol Substitution score and FNAME-total. The four components were converted into z-scores based on the performance of the full Insight 46 sample at phase 1 and the four z-scores were then averaged. This was also calculated at phase 1 - see library file I46_neuropsychology.
I46P2_neuropsychology Sub-Studies Insight46 Cognitive function Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) paccnum_i46p2 61651 Number of PACC sub-tests completed (normally 4 unless there is some missing data) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 No No Insight46 phase 2 Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) - The four components of our version of the PACC were: MMSE total score, Logical Memory delayed recall score, Digit-Symbol Substitution score and FNAME-total. The four components were converted into z-scores based on the performance of the full Insight 46 sample at phase 1 and the four z-scores were then averaged. This was also calculated at phase 1 - see library file I46_neuropsychology.

I46_brainVolumes Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain volumes Brain volumes - Cross-sectional brain_vol_i46p1 60008 Total brain volume (cm3) - Cross-sectional - Insight46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown cm3 Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 brain volumes measured cross-sectionally from T1 MRI. Not to be used to be used for longitudinal analysis with phase 2 brain volumes. There are separate variables for longitudinal comparison of brain volume between phases 1 and 2 - see library file I46 brainVolumes_L and I46P2_brainVolumes_L.

I46_brainVolumes_L Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain volumes Brain volumes - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2) brain_vol_l_i46 60165 Total brain volume (cm3) - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2 only) - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown cm3 Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 brain volumes measured from T1 MRI as part of the longitudinal pipeline. Can be used for longitudinal analysis with phase 2 brain volumes. Note that boundary shift integral measures have also been quantified for some regions (whole brain, ventricles, hippocampus) which provide more precise estimates of volume change between phase 1 and 2 compared to indirectly calculating the difference in volume between each time-point - see library file - I46P2_brainVolumes_L
I46P2_brainVolumes_L Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI Brain volumes Brain volumes - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2) brain_vol_l_i46p2 60165 Total brain volume (cm3) - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2 only) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown cm3 Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 2 brain volumes measured from T1 MRI as part of the longitudinal pipeline. Note that boundary shift integral measures have been quantified for some regions (whole brain, ventricles, hippocampus) which provide more precise estimates of volume change between phase 1 and 2 compared to indirectly calculating the difference in volume between each time-point. For phase 1 brain volumes measured from T1 MRI as part of longitudinal pipeline - see library file - I46_brainVolumes_L.

I46P2_Bamos Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional globalwmhv_i46p2 61925 Global white matter hyperintensity (WMH) volume - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown ml Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No This data is intended for Phase 2 cross-sectional analysis only, and should not be used in combination with Phase 1 cross-sectional WMHV data to calculate change in WMHV. For longitudinal analysis of WMHV change between Phase 1 and Phase 2, we recommend using bamos_wmhvc_i46p2 which was produced from a different longitudinal pipeline.

I46P2_Bamos2 Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional mult_sclerosis_i46p2 62823 Evidence of multiple sclerosis (MS) - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes Yes Potential low cell counts issue. This data is intended for Phase 2 cross-sectional analysis only, and should not be used in combination with Phase 1 cross-sectional WMHV data to calculate change in WMHV. For longitudinal analysis of WMHV change between Phase 1 and Phase 2, we recommend using bamos_wmhvc_i46p2 which was produced from a different longitudinal pipeline.
I46_bamos Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional distwmhbg_i46p1 60188 Proportion of global white matter hyperintensity (WMH) in the basal ganglia (value 0 – 1) - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Unknown / no data Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volumes (global and regional) generated using the cross-sectional Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline . Note that there is also a longitudinal phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volume dataset which was generated after phase 2 data collection was complete - library file I46P2_Bamos_L.

I46_bamos Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional lesiontot_i46p1 60197 Global white matter hyperintensity (WMH) volume (ml). Includes subcortical grey matter but not the infratentorial region - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} Unknown / no data ml Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volumes (global and regional) generated using the cross-sectional Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline . Note that there is also a longitudinal phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volume dataset which was generated after phase 2 data collection was complete - library file I46P2_Bamos_L. For longitudinal analysis of WMH volume change, we recommend using the I46P2_Bamos_L dataset rather than a combination of cross-sectional measurements of WMH volume from Phase 1 and Phase 2. This is because WMH volumes calculated in a longitudinal framework from mid-point registered images tend to be a more conservative estimate of WMH volume, incorporating information from both time-points.
