I46_bamos Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional bamosuseable_i46p1 60187 Whether the white matter hyperintensity (WMH) segmentation is useable - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} No data {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volumes (global and regional) generated using the cross-sectional Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline . Note that there is also a longitudinal phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volume dataset which was generated after phase 2 data collection was complete - library file I46P2_Bamos_L.
I46_bamos Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional bamosfailreason_i46p1 60186 Reason why a segmentation was not useable (only relevant if bamosuseable_i46p1=0) - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {-99.0} No failure {1.0} T1 fail {2.0} FLAIR fail {3.0} Bamos segmentation fail {4.0} Cortical stroke mis-segmented {5.0} Demyelination mis-segmented {6.0} Other vascular anomaly mis-segmented Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Insight46 phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volumes (global and regional) generated using the cross-sectional Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline. Note that there is also a longitudinal phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volume dataset which was generated after phase 2 data collection was complete - library file I46P2_Bamos_L.
I46P2_Bamos_L Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain MRI White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2) bamos_wmhvc_i46p2 60214 Change in white matter hyperintensity (WMH) volume from baseline to follow-up (p2_bamos_wmhv_long – p1_bamos_wmhv_long) - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2 only) - Insight46 phase 2 {-9.0} Unknown ml Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 & 2 white matter hyperintensity volumes generated using the longitudinal Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline. These data are intended for longitudinal analysis, and should be used to reflect WMH volume change between Phase 1 and Phase 2. For cross-sectional analysis of Phase 1 global WMH volume, use the variable lesiontot_i46p1. For cross-sectional analysis using only Phase 2 WMH volumes, use the cross-sectional Phase 2 WMHV variable. This is because WMH volumes calculated in a longitudinal framework from mid-point registered images tend to be a more conservative estimate of WMH volume, incorporating information from both time-points. If only interested in WMH volume at a single time-point, cross-sectional measurements are more appropriate. For longitudinal analysis of WMH volume change, it is recommended that only this dataset is used, rather than a combination of cross-sectional measurements of WMH volume from Phase 1 and Phase 2. For phase 1 cross-sectional measurements, see file handle I46_bamos.
I46_amyloidstatus_crb Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) suvr_crb_i46p1 60243 Global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVR) with whole cerebellum reference region - Insight 46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to a whole cerebellum reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (non-PVC > 1.0770 and PVC > 1.0306). See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_erodedWM for SUVR and amyloid-beta status calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only. For longitudinal pipelines (Phase 1 baseline and Phase 2 follow-up), see file handle I46P2_AmyloidPET for longitudinal values.
I46_amyloidstatus_crb Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) suvrpvc_crb_i46p1 60244 Global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVR) with with whole cerebrellum reference region with partial volume correction (PVC) - Insight 46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to a whole cerebellum reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (non-PVC > 1.0770 and PVC > 1.0306). See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_erodedWM for SUVR and amyloid-beta status calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only. For longitudinal pipelines (Phase 1 baseline and Phase 2 follow-up), see file handle I46P2_AmyloidPET for longitudinal values.

Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (non-PVC > 1.0770 and PVC > 1.0306). See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_erodedWM for SUVR and amyloid-beta status calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only. For longitudinal pipelines (Phase 1 baseline and Phase 2 follow-up), see file handle I46P2_AmyloidPET for longitudinal values.

Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (non-PVC > 1.0770 and PVC > 1.0306). See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_erodedWM for SUVR and amyloid-beta status calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only. I46_amyloidstatus_crb Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) amyld_pvcst_i46p1 60248 Amyloid-beta status with whole cerebellum reference region with partial volume correction (PVC) - Insight 46 phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} Negative {1.0} Positive Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to a whole cerebellum reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (non-PVC > 1.0770 and PVC > 1.0306). See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_erodedWM for SUVR and amyloid-beta status calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only. For longitudinal pipelines (Phase 1 baseline and Phase 2 follow-up), see file handle I46P2_AmyloidPET for longitudinal values.
I46_amyloidstatus_erodedwm Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) suvr_i46p1 60249 Global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVR) with eroded white matter (WM) reference region - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique.Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (0·6104). Insight 46 Phase 1. See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum for values calculated using a whole cerebellum reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only. For longitudinal pipelines (Phase 1 baseline and Phase 2 follow-up), see file handle I46P2_AmyloidPET for longitudinal values.
I46_amyloidstatus_erodedwm Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) amyloidstatus_i46p1 60250 Amyloid-beta status with eroded white matter (WM) reference region - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} Negative {1.0} Positive Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique.Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (0·6104). Insight 46 Phase 1. Also calculated for phase 2.
I46_amyloidstatus_erodedwm Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) suvrpvc_i46p1 60251 Global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVR) with eroded white matter (WM) reference region with partial volume correction (PVC) - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique.Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (0·6104). Insight 46 Phase 1. Also calculated for phase 2.
I46_amyloidstatus_erodedwm Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) pvcamyloidstatus_i46p1 60252 Amyloid-beta status with eroded white matter (WM) reference region with partial volume correction (PVC) - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} Negative {1.0} Positive Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 Yes No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique.Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (0·6104). Insight 46 Phase 1. Also calculated for phase 2.
I46_amyloidstatus_erodedwm Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) imp_amyloid_i46p1 60253 Imputed amyloid-beta status with eroded white matter (WM) reference region - Insight 46 Phase 1 {-9.0} Unknown {0.0} No {1.0} Yes Not applicable Insight46 - Phase 1 Not applicable 2015-2018 No No Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique.Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (0·6104). Insight 46 Phase 1. Also calculated for phase 2.
I46P2_AmyloidPET Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Longitudinal beta-amyloid status (phase 1 and 2) ce_cmp_wc_bl_i46p2 60255 Centiloid value from SUVRs using composite target region with whole cerebellum reference region - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2 only) - Insight46 phase 1 {-888.0} Not applicable {-9.0} Unknown CL Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 & 2 amyloid-beta PET [18F]florbetapir data quantified using longitudinal pipeline. Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.
I46P2_AmyloidPET Sub-Studies Insight46 Brain PET Amyloid data Longitudinal beta-amyloid status (phase 1 and 2) ce_cmp_wc_fu_i46p2 60256 Centiloid value from SUVRs using composite target region with whole cerebellum reference region - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2 only) - Insight46 phase 2 {-888.0} Not applicable {-9.0} Unknown CL Insight46 - Phase 2 Not applicable 2018-2021 Yes No Insight46 phase 1 & 2 amyloid-beta PET [18F]florbetapir data quantified using longitudinal pipeline. Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum.
